_instructions.how_to_use: Fill in one or more entities in the 'entities' array below. Delete this _instructions block before sending back. Each field is explained in _field_guide. Include both histology and cytology entities in the same array — the 'type' field distinguishes them.

SHARED FIELDS (all entities).id: Unique identifier — lowercase, no spaces, format 'entityname-001'. E.g. 'fsgs-001', 'thy-ptc-cyto-001'.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).name: Full entity name in CAPITALS. E.g. 'FOCAL SEGMENTAL GLOMERULOSCLEROSIS (FSGS)'.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).subspecialty: Choose one: LGI | UGI | LGI / UGI | Hepatobiliary | Haempath | Medical Renal | Gynaecological | Thoracic | Neuropath | Skin / Dermatopath | Head & Neck | Breast | Soft Tissue / Bone | Urological
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).behaviour: Choose one: Benign | Intermediate | Malignant | Variable
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).ihcPos: Array of strings. Format: 'MARKER - sensitivity% or descriptor'. E.g. 'CD117 - 95%', 'SOX10 - diffuse'. Use ['N/A - morphological diagnosis'] if IHC not applicable.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).ihcNeg: Array of strings. Key exclusions only. Same format.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).differentials: Array of objects. Each has 'dx' (differential name) and 'disc' (what discriminates). Aim for 3.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).comments: Array of strings. Molecular alterations, grading, staging pearls, management, prognosis, syndromic associations. One point per entry.
SHARED FIELDS (all entities).related: Array of entity IDs already in the codex. Leave as [] if none.

HISTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: histology).pattern: Medical Renal: Nephritic | Nephrotic | RPGN | Thrombotic microangiopathy | Tubular | Tubulointerstitial | Vascular | Sclerosing. Medical Liver: Hepatitic | Cholestatic | Steatotic | Fibrotic/cirrhotic | Granulomatous | Vascular | Massive necrosis. Inflammatory Skin: Lichenoid | Spongiotic | Interface | Psoriasiform | Bullous | Granulomatous | Vasculitic | Neutrophilic | Perivascular. Solid tumours: Spindle cell | Epithelioid | Myxoid | Fibrous/collagenous | Glandular/mucinous | Mucinous | Neuroendocrine | Round cell | Biphasic | Perivascular | Amphicrine | Mixed.
HISTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: histology).clinical: Array of strings. Age/sex predilection, site, associations, serology/labs, classic trigger phrases.
HISTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: histology).micro: Array of strings. Aim for 3. Low-power architecture first, then high-power cytology, then characteristic/discriminating feature.
HISTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: histology).cytoLink: ID of corresponding cytology entity in the codex. Use null if none.

CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).specimenType: Choose one: FNA | Effusion | Urine | CSF | Sputum | Brushings | LBC | Bile duct | Other
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).reportingSystem: Choose one: RCPath Thyroid (Thy1-5) | Milan (Salivary) | Paris (Urine) | IAC Harrogate (Effusion) | Descriptive
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).reportingCategory: The specific category assigned. E.g. 'Thy3a', 'Thy4', 'Milan 4b', 'Paris SHGUC', 'IAC Suspicious'.
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).adequacy: Array of strings. Minimum cellularity criteria, technical requirements, what to report when inadequate.
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).cytologicalFeatures: Array of strings. Aim for 4: cellularity/arrangement, nuclear features (size, N:C, chromatin, nucleoli), cytoplasmic features, background.
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).pitfalls: Array of strings. Preparation/fixation artefacts, sampling pitfalls, morphological mimics specific to cytological preparations.
CYTOLOGY-ONLY FIELDS (type: cytology).histoLink: ID of corresponding histology entity in the codex. Use null if none.

entities._comment: EXAMPLE HISTOLOGY ENTITY - delete before returning
entities.name: FOCAL SEGMENTAL GLOMERULOSCLEROSIS (FSGS)
clinical: Most common cause of nephrotic syndrome in adults (particularly Black patients).
Primary: podocyte injury via circulating permeability factor. Secondary: hyperfiltration (obesity, solitary kidney), drug-induced (heroin, pamidronate), viral (HIV), genetic (NPHS2, ACTN4).
Primary presents with full nephrotic syndrome; secondary often sub-nephrotic proteinuria + CKD.
Trigger: nephrotic syndrome + segmental sclerosis on biopsy - classify Columbia variant before treatment.
micro: Focal (some glomeruli) and segmental (part of tuft) sclerosis with hyalinosis; obliteration of capillary lumina; foam cells; adhesions to Bowman's capsule.

Columbia classification: NOS | Perihilar (secondary/adaptive) | Cellular (endocapillary hypercellularity) | Tip (urinary pole - best prognosis) | Collapsing (global collapse + podocyte hyperplasia - worst; HIV/viral).
EM: foot process effacement (diffuse = primary; focal = secondary). IF: non-specific IgM/C3 in sclerotic segments only - pauci-immune.

differentials.disc: No segmental sclerosis on LM; diffuse FPE on EM; steroid-responsive
Perihilar variant; focal FPE on EM; sub-nephrotic; identifiable underlying cause
Mesangial IgA on IF; mesangial hypercellularity; haematuria predominant

comments: Columbia classification mandatory - variant determines prognosis and treatment.
Collapsing FSGS: HIV (HIVAN); COVID-19; pamidronate. APOL1 G1/G2 risk alleles in Black patients (7-10x increased risk). Worst prognosis.
Genetic FSGS: NPHS2 (podocin, AR) - childhood/young adult, steroid-resistant.
Treatment: high-dose steroids (primary); CNI for steroid-resistant; rituximab second-line. Treat underlying cause (secondary).
Post-transplant recurrence: primary FSGS ~30-40%; plasmapheresis + rituximab.

entities._comment: EXAMPLE CYTOLOGY ENTITY - delete before returning
entities.name: PAPILLARY THYROID CARCINOMA - FNA (Thy4/Thy5)
adequacy: Minimum 6 groups of >=10 well-preserved follicular cells on at least 2 slides (RCPath standard).
Cystic lesions: adequacy assessed on cellular component, not fluid. Report Thy1c if cystic and acellular.
cytologicalFeatures: Papillary fronds with fibrovascular cores; monolayer sheets with crowding and nuclear overlap - low-power architecture.
Nuclear enlargement; irregular membranes (grooves, pseudoinclusions); fine powdery chromatin; ground-glass nuclei - nuclear features.
Background: dense 'bubblegum' colloid; psammoma bodies; multinucleated giant cells.

differentials.disc: Microfollicular pattern; no PTC nuclear features; no papillae; needs histological excision
Calcitonin+, CEA+, Synaptophysin+; plasmacytoid/spindle cells; amyloid; no PTC nuclei
Flame cells; watery colloid; no nuclear grooves/inclusions; no papillary architecture

pitfalls: Nuclear pseudoinclusions less reliable in FNA than histology (cytoplasmic invagination artefact) - nuclear grooves more useful.
Cystic PTC: heavily cystic aspirates show only macrophages - sample solid areas; Thy1c if acellular.
BRAF V600E IHC on cell block: highly specific but negative in wild-type and non-classical variants.
comments: Thy4 = suspicious (~60-75% malignancy risk); Thy5 = malignant (>95%).
BRAF V600E: ~80% classical PTC; associated with extrathyroidal extension, LN mets, radioiodine resistance.
Classical PTC: excellent prognosis (10-year OS >95%). Tall cell/hobnail/diffuse sclerosing variants - worse.
